mnemonics: V.I.N.D.I.C.A.T.E. Your Setup: Verify, Install, Node, Dependencies, IDE, Configure, APIs, Test, Environment
keyPoints: Always verify system requirements and compatibility before installing any new software or frameworks.
Use official package managers (like npm, pip) and version managers (like nvm) to ensure clean, consistent, and reproducible installations.
A successful installation is confirmed by running a simple 'Hello World' test or the framework's built-in verification command (e.g., `node -v`, `django-admin --version`).
commonMistakes: Not checking for existing installations or conflicting versions, leading to 'version hell' and broken environments.
Skipping the step of installing project-specific dependencies after cloning a repo, resulting in ModuleNotFound errors.
Installing packages globally when they should be local to the project (or vice-versa), causing permission issues or deployment failures.
